34: **Ecuador** are strong favorites against tournament debutants **Curaçao**, the smallest nation ever to reach this stage. La Tri's miserly defense was among the stingiest in qualifying, and they'll look to control the game and ease toward the knockout rounds — while **Curaçao**, with nothing to lose, will sit deep and hunt a famous counter-attacking moment.
36: **Japan**, one of Asia's most polished and well-organized sides, meet a physical **Tunisia** team that thrives on set pieces and defensive discipline. The Samurai Blue's quick, technical passing will test the Carthage Eagles, who'll try to slow the tempo and strike on the break. With every point precious in a tight group, neither side can afford a slow start.
37: **Uruguay** bring experience, grit and genuine quality against **Cabo Verde**, the islanders savoring a historic first appearance on this stage. La Celeste are clear favorites and will want a commanding opening result, but **Cabo Verde** have been fearless throughout their run and won't be overawed by the occasion.
38: World No. 2 **Spain** open as heavy favorites, but **Saudi Arabia** have a well-earned reputation for springing tournament shocks. Expect La Roja to dominate possession and probe patiently; the Green Falcons will stay compact, defend in numbers and look to punish any complacency on the counter.
39: **Belgium**'s talented generation faces a disciplined, deep-defending **IR Iran** side that is among Asia's hardest teams to break down. The Red Devils have the individual class to unlock almost anyone, but patience and precision will be essential against a team built to frustrate and counter.
40: **Egypt**, led by their star forward, carry far more attacking firepower than **New Zealand**, the lowest-ranked team in the group. The All Whites will defend deep and try to make life uncomfortable for the Pharaohs, but **Egypt** are firm favorites to begin their campaign with three points.
